briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposed AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse, raising urgent questions about consumer protection in embedded banking.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign payment network, signalling a major geopolitical shift away from US-dominated card infrastructure.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the global payments landscape.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of open banking firm Credit Kudos signals Big Tech's growing ambitions in financial data and alternative credit decisioning.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Would you give an AI agent your credit card? Companies are betting so](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/ai-shopping-agentic-payments-9.7222389?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian and global companies are racing to build agentic payment systems that allow AI to autonomously make purchases on consumers' behalf, raising new questions about trust, liability, and financial oversight.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to a timeline for implementing a formal open banking framework, a regulatory journey still relevant to today's ecosystem builders.

- **[Deep Sky announces carbon capture agreement with TD Bank](https://betakit.com/deep-sky-announces-carbon-capture-agreement-with-td-bank/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian carbon capture startup Deep Sky has secured a landmark agreement with TD Bank, highlighting the growing intersection of sustainable finance and Canadian fintech innovation.

- **[Inovia promotes Mia Morisset to partner on growth team](https://betakit.com/inovia-promotes-mia-morisset-to-partner-on-growth-team/)** — *BetaKit* — Leading Canadian venture firm Inovia Capital has elevated Mia Morisset to partner on its growth team, signalling continued investment in scaling Canadian tech and fintech companies.

- **[Canadian economy adds 88,000 jobs in May, helping offset job losses so far this year](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-jobs-may-2026-9.7224553?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's labour market posted a strong rebound in May with 88,000 new jobs, providing a positive macroeconomic backdrop for fintech lending, payments, and consumer financial services.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Why scaling requires more than speed](https://betakit.com/why-scaling-requires-more-than-speed/)** — *BetaKit* — A timely exploration of what sustainable startup scaling actually looks like, offering key lessons for Atlantic Canadian fintech founders navigating growth beyond early-stage momentum.

- **[Canada's AI strategy draws mixed reviews from across tech ecosystem](https://betakit.com/canadas-ai-strategy-draws-mixed-reviews-from-across-the-tech-ecosystem/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's newly released AI strategy is generating debate across the tech sector, with implications for how Atlantic Canadian fintech and AI startups access federal support and talent pipelines.

- **[Tentative agreement reached for striking long term care workers](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/tentative-agreement-reached-for-striking-long-term-care-workers)** — *Financial Post* — A tentative labour deal was reached in Halifax between CUPE Local 1082 and St. Vincent's Nursing Home, a reminder of the growing demand for digital payroll, benefits, and financial wellness tools serving Nova Scotia's frontline workforce.
